title: The Context Window Limit
concept: Every LLM has a maximum token capacity (the context window); exceeding this limit cuts off early inputs, causing the model to forget context.

exercises.prompt: Can an AI chatbot remember a conversation that is millions of pages through-the-chat AI models remember an infinite amount of text during a single conversation?

options.text: No, they have a strict memory limit.
Yes, but only if you type in ALL CAPS.

exercises.explain: LLMs have a limited memory buffer called a context window, which restricts how much text they can read at one time.

cards.title: Why Do AIs Forget?
cards.body: Have you ever talked to an AI and noticed it suddenly forgot what you were talking about just ten minutes ago? It didn't get bored—it ran out of memory space! Let's find out why this happens.

cards.title: What is the Context Window?
cards.body: Every AI model has a maximum 'context window'. This is the total amount of text (tokens) it can read and remember at one time. If you feed it more text than this limit, it starts 'forgetting' the oldest parts of your conversation.

cards.title: The Writing Desk
cards.body: Imagine your brain is a small writing desk. You can only lay out a few pages of notes at once. If you want to read a new page, you have to push an older page off the edge of the desk. The desk's size is the context window—once it's full, old details fall off and are forgotten!

cards.title: Memory Loss in Action
cards.body: If you paste a giant 100-page document into a chat with a small context window, and then ask: 'What was the first sentence of this document?', the AI might guess or say it doesn't know. The beginning of the document literally fell out of its memory limit!

cards.title: Myth: Infinite Memory
cards.body: Common Myth: People think AIs have infinite memory and can read entire libraries in a single prompt. Reality: Every model has a strict limit. If you cross that limit, the AI becomes blind to the earliest parts of your chat.

cards.title: The Memory Conveyor Belt
cards.body: Think of the context window like a scrolling banner. As new words enter, the oldest words must slide off the screen.

left.text: Enters the window, making the AI focus on the latest messages.

right.text: Pushed out of the window and completely forgotten by the AI.

exercises.prompt: What does the term 'context window' refer to in LLMs?

options.text: The number of seconds the AI takes to reply.
The maximum amount of text the AI can read and remember at once.
The size of the window on your computer screen.
The speed of the internet connection.

exercises.explain: The context window represents the maximum limit of text (tokens) that the model can process and remember at any one time.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you exceed the context window, the AI will permanently lose access to the very first messages you sent in that chat.
exercises.explain: Once the context window limit is exceeded, the oldest tokens are discarded to make room for new ones, meaning the AI can no longer see them.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence about context window overflow.
exercises.sentence: Exceeding the context window limit causes the AI to _______ older parts of the conversation.
exercises.explain: When you feed too much text to an LLM, the oldest parts of the chat are pushed out of the context window, causing the model to forget them.

exercises.prompt: Which AI response shows signs of running out of its context window?

options.text: AI A: 'Hello! I forgot your name, can you remind me who you are?' (after a long chat)
AI B: 'Hello Sarah! I see we have written a lot of code today.' (after a long chat)
Neither show signs of memory loss.

exercises.explain: AI A shows signs of exceeding its context window because it forgot the user's name mentioned at the very beginning of the chat. AI B successfully remembered the context.

exercises.prompt: You start a chat by giving an LLM strict rules, then you paste a massive 50-page book into the chat. Why does the LLM suddenly ignore your original rules?

options.text: The LLM gets tired and stops responding altogether.
It forgets the initial instructions because they got pushed out of the context window.
The LLM's computer overheats and shuts down.
The LLM automatically compresses the text into emojis.

exercises.explain: Exceeding the context window limit forces the model to drop the oldest information (your initial instructions) to make room for the new text, leading to forgotten rules.
